title: Why do so many algo strategies fail after going live?
author: Cold-Pin-345
selftext:
Something I’ve noticed after years messing with EAs:

Most strategies don’t explode immediately.

They slowly die.

Winrate drops a bit.  
Drawdown increases slightly.  
Entries still look “correct.”

Nothing feels obviously broken — until months later the edge is gone.

It made me wonder if the real challenge in algo trading isn’t building strategies…

…but recognizing when they quietly stop working.

How do you personally detect strategy decay before serious losses happen?

comments.author: DayNo4131
comments.body: false edge discovery during backtesting. When you run millions of parameter combinations, then optimize for maximum return / Sharpe and select the best-performing curve, youare **guaranteed** to pick a **statistical accident**.

comments.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
comments.body:
honestly this is the part nobody talks about enough, the slow death is way harder to catch than a blowup.

what i do is track rolling PF and winrate against my backtest baseline, if it's consistently drifting below for 20-30 trades that's when i start paying attention. not one bad week, consistent drift over time.

the hardest part is knowing if it's just variance or actual decay. that's where most people mess up, according to me, they either panic pull it too early or hold it way too long hoping it recovers.
